messages.content: rewrite "In this section we describe the methods proposed to reduce the size for each component of the original MusicGen-Small models." for technical paper
In this section, we will outline the proposed methods for reducing the size of each component in the original MusicGen-Small models.
rewrite "as shown in Figure" for techincal paper
as illustrated in Figure

rewrite "In this section we describe the proposed methods for reducing the size of each component (as illustrated in Figure \ref{fig:main_diagram}) in the original MusicGen-Small model." for techinical paper
This section outlines the proposed methods for reducing the size of each component within the original MusicGen-Small model, as depicted in Figure \ref{fig:main_diagram}.

rewrite "The T5 model architecture is available in different sizes \cite{tay2022scale} while in this paper we mainly focus on T5-tiny (used in TinyMusicGen) and T5-base (used in MusicGen-small).
In TinyMusicGen, despite the fact that we exploit only the encoder part of T5, we decided to train with the entire model taking advantage of span-based MLM. We experimented primarily with T5-tiny carrying out a series of experiments. Concerning model size reduction, we merely removed some encoder layers. The analysis of the experiments conducted is presented in Section \ref{sec:encoderAnalysis}." for technical paper

The T5 model architecture is offered in various sizes \cite{tay2022scale}. This paper focuses predominantly on T5-tiny (utilized in TinyMusicGen) and T5-base (implemented in MusicGen-small). In TinyMusicGen, despite utilizing only the encoder section of T5, the decision was made to train the entire model using span-based MLM. Our primary experimental focus was on T5-tiny, where a series of experiments were conducted. To reduce the model size, we opted to remove certain encoder layers. The analysis of these experiments is presented in Section \ref{sec:encoderAnalysis}.

rewrite "The T5 model architecture is offered in various sizes \cite{tay2022scale}. This paper focuses predominantly on T5-tiny (utilized in TinyMusicGen) and compares the performance with T5-base (implemented in MusicGen-small)." for techinical paper

The T5 model architecture is available in multiple sizes \cite{tay2022scale}. This paper primarily delves into T5-tiny (utilized in TinyMusicGen) and conducts performance comparisons with T5-base (implemented in MusicGen-small).

rewrite "The T5-tiny pretrained mode is directly integrated in the music generation pipeline but also we explore finetuning it on text with music descriptions. In order to finetune the model we leverage the span-based MLM objective and cross-entropy loss. Additionally, to further reduce the model we drop last encoder layers from the initial 4 layers." for technical paper

The T5-tiny pretrained model is seamlessly integrated into the music generation pipeline. Moreover, we investigate its fine-tuning on textual data containing music descriptions. To achieve this, we utilize the span-based MLM objective and cross-entropy loss. Furthermore, for additional model reduction, we eliminate the last encoder layers, starting from the fourth layer.

rewrite “The T5-tiny pretrained mode is directly integrated in the music generation pipeline but also we explore finetuning it on text with music descriptions. In order to finetune the model we leverage the span-based MLM objective and cross-entropy loss. Additionally, to further reduce the model we drop last encoder layers from the initial 4 layers.” for technical paper

The T5-tiny pretrained model is seamlessly integrated into the music generation pipeline and further explored for fine-tuning on text containing music descriptions. Fine-tuning efforts leverage the span-based MLM objective and cross-entropy loss mechanisms. In addition, the model size reduction strategy involves omitting the last encoder layers starting from the initial four layers.
rewrite "We investigate freezing only the decoder layers while training since the decoder layers are not employed for inference. "
We explore the concept of freezing solely the decoder layers during training, given that these layers are not utilized for inference purposes.
rewrite "We investigate freezing only the decoder layers while training since the decoder layers are not employed for inference. " technical paper
During our investigation, we specifically explore the strategy of freezing only the decoder layers during training, as these layers are not utilized during inference.

summarize "In details, Encodec utilises a streaming, convolutional-based encoder-decoder architecture. The encoder and the decoder components incorporate sequential modeling over the latent representation. The encoder part consists of 1D convolution with 64 channels and a kernel size of 7 followed by 4 convolution blocks. Each convolution block consists of a single residual unit followed by a down-sampling layer leading in a strided convolution, with a kernel size K of twice the stride S. The corresponding strides S are (4,4,5,8). Two convolutions with a kernel size of 3 and a skip-connection are present in the residual unit.  In every downsampling the number of channels is doubled. Following the convolution blocks, there is a two-layer LSTM for sequence modeling and a final 1D convolution layer with a kernel size of 7 and 128 output channels. The decoder mirrors the encoder architecture but in the reverse direction, decoding the latent representation to generate the output audio." in 3 sentences for technical paper

Encodec utilizes a streaming architecture with a convolutional-based encoder-decoder design. The encoder employs sequential modeling using 1D convolutions, 4 convolution blocks, down-sampling layers, skip-connections, LSTM units, and a final 1D convolution layer. The decoder, following a similar architecture in reverse, transforms the latent representation to generate the output audio.

summarize “In details, Encodec utilises a streaming, convolutional-based encoder-decoder architecture. The encoder and the decoder components incorporate sequential modeling over the latent representation. The encoder part consists of 1D convolution with 64 channels and a kernel size of 7 followed by 4 convolution blocks. Each convolution block consists of a single residual unit followed by a down-sampling layer leading in a strided convolution, with a kernel size K of twice the stride S. The corresponding strides S are (4,4,5,8). Two convolutions with a kernel size of 3 and a skip-connection are present in the residual unit. In every downsampling the number of channels is doubled. Following the convolution blocks, there is a two-layer LSTM for sequence modeling and a final 1D convolution layer with a kernel size of 7 and 128 output channels. The decoder mirrors the encoder architecture but in the reverse direction, decoding the latent representation to generate the output audio.” in4 sentences for technical paper

Encodec features a streaming, convolutional-based encoder-decoder architecture, with sequential modeling applied to the latent representation. The encoder segment includes 1D convolution with 64 channels, 4 convolution blocks with residual units and down-sampling layers, as well as two-layer LSTM and final 1D convolution. The decoder, mirroring the encoder design in reverse, transforms the latent representation into the output audio. Each downsampling step doubles the number of channels to enhance model capabilities.

summarize “In details, Encodec utilises a streaming, convolutional-based encoder-decoder architecture. The encoder and the decoder components incorporate sequential modeling over the latent representation. The encoder part consists of 1D convolution with 64 channels and a kernel size of 7 followed by 4 convolution blocks. Each convolution block consists of a single residual unit followed by a down-sampling layer leading in a strided convolution, with a kernel size K of twice the stride S. The corresponding strides S are (4,4,5,8). Two convolutions with a kernel size of 3 and a skip-connection are present in the residual unit. In every downsampling the number of channels is doubled. Following the convolution blocks, there is a two-layer LSTM for sequence modeling and a final 1D convolution layer with a kernel size of 7 and 128 output channels. The decoder mirrors the encoder architecture but in the reverse direction, decoding the latent representation to generate the output audio.” in 5 sentences for technical paper

Encodec employs a streaming, convolutional-based encoder-decoder architecture with sequential modeling over the latent representation. The encoder section utilizes 1D convolution with 64 channels, 4 convolution blocks with residual units and down-sampling layers. Down-sampling involves a stride-convolution process with specific kernel sizes and corresponding strides. The encoder also incorporates two convolutions with skip-connections and a two-layer LSTM for sequence modeling, concluding with a final 1D convolution layer. The decoder, structured in reverse of the encoder, translates the latent representation into the final output audio.
rewrite "The Encodec is trained using a combination of different loss functions. " for technical paper
The training of Encodec involves the utilization of a variety of loss functions in combination.

rewrite "The distillation is applied only in the decoder part, since the encoder and the quantizer parts shall remain the same, to avoid teacher-student token mismatch in the MusicGen token generation model. We selected to decrease only the number of filters in the convolution kernels in order to reduce the number of parameters. Experiments using only one LSTM layer without modifying the number of filters was also explored however the resulting size of the decoder was relatively hie (20M parameters)." for technical paper

Distillation is exclusively implemented in the decoder section to maintain consistency in the encoder and quantizer segments, preventing potential token mismatch in the MusicGen token generation model. The decision was made to solely decrease the number of filters in the convolution kernels, aiming to reduce parameter count. Although experiments involving a single LSTM layer without altering the filter numbers were conducted, it led to a relatively high size of the decoder (20M parameters).
rewrite "It has to be noted here that the Discriminator was trained with soft and hard labels" for technical paper
It is important to mention that the Discriminator was trained using both soft and hard labels.
rewrite “It has to be noted here that the Discriminator was trained with soft and hard labels” for technical paper
It should be emphasized that the Discriminator was trained using a combination of soft and hard labels.
rewrte "The scores shown in Table \ref{tab:T5_results} are the average of five different generations with different seeds." for technical paper
The scores presented in Table \ref{tab:T5_results} represent the average of five distinct generations generated using different seeds.

rewrite "This table indicates that T5-tiny models fine-tuned with three and two encoder layers compare to only fine-tune show small differences, indicating that the reduction from three to two layers does not significantly impact the performance of MusicGen." for technical papers

This table suggests that fine-tuning T5-tiny models with either three or two encoder layers yields comparable results, indicating minimal performance differences between the two. This observation implies that reducing the number of layers from three to two has little impact on MusicGen's overall performance.

rewrite "The scores presented in Table \ref{tab:T5_results} represent the average of five distinct generations generated using different seeds. This table  firstly indicates the large performance gap between the default T5-base and T5-tiny when applied to TTM. Furthermore, fine-tuning the T5-tiny improves the results, reducing both the FAD and KL scores. However, the T5-base default configuration still achieve lower scores at the cost of larger model size (approx. a factor of 10). Fine-tuning the T5-tiny with a frozen decoder does not seem to yield any competitive result, likely due to the fewer trainable parameters. Finally,  T5-tiny models fine-tuned with three or two encoder layers show negligible differences, but both variants subtly deteriorate the performance metrics to the initial T5-tiny fine-tuned which comprises 4 layers, suggesting that there is a trade-off between model parameters and efficacy." for technical paper

The scores in Table \ref{tab:T5_results} are averages from five different generations generated with varying seeds. This table highlights the notable performance contrast between the standard T5-base and T5-tiny models in TTM. Fine-tuning the T5-tiny model leads to enhanced results, evidenced by decreased FAD and KL scores. Despite this improvement, the T5-base default configuration outperforms the fine-tuned T5-tiny model, albeit with a larger model size (approximately tenfold). Fine-tuning the T5-tiny model with a fixed decoder does not produce competitive results, potentially due to the limited trainable parameters. Notably, fine-tuning T5-tiny models with three or two encoder layers results in minimal performance differences compared to the initial T5-tiny model with four layers, suggesting a trade-off between model parameters and effectiveness.
